title: Maternal Cardiac Arrest (Code Blue) in Pregnancy: Team Leadership and Pregnancy-Modified Resuscitation
subtitle: A 34-year-old at 36 weeks collapses on the antepartum unit and is pulseless. The nurse must lead a code with pregnancy modifications — continuous manual left uterine displacement, standard ACLS, and readiness for a perimortem cesarean if there is no ROSC.

patient.name: Renata Cole
patient.admitDate: G1 at 36 weeks, admitted for observation. Witnessed sudden collapse — unresponsive, apneic, pulseless. Suspected pulmonary/amniotic-fluid embolism. The nurse activates a code and leads resuscitation with pregnancy modifications while OB, anesthesia, and neonatal teams are summoned.

labs.r: Evaluate hemorrhage as a reversible cause.
If Mg toxicity: give IV calcium gluconate.

nursesNote: Renata Cole, 34, G1 36 weeks, witnessed collapse — unresponsive, apneic, pulseless. Code activated. CPR started with continuous manual left uterine displacement. OB, anesthesia, and neonatal teams paged. Defibrillator and crash cart at bedside. Preparing for perimortem cesarean if no ROSC by ~4 minutes.
providerOrders: Run ACLS with standard drug doses and defibrillation energies
Continuous manual left uterine displacement throughout resuscitation
Perimortem cesarean at bedside if no ROSC by ~4 minutes (deliver by ~5)
Notify OB, anesthesia, and neonatal/NICU teams immediately
Identify/treat reversible causes (H's and T's, hemorrhage, embolism, eclampsia, Mg toxicity)

opts.text: Check responsiveness and pulse; if pulseless, call a code and start CPR
Because she is ≥20 weeks, provide continuous manual LEFT UTERINE DISPLACEMENT during CPR
Delay compressions until the OB team arrives
Apply 100% oxygen and prepare for early advanced airway
Summon OB and neonatal/NICU teams in addition to the code team
opts.rat: Prepare for delivery and the newborn.

opts.text: Remove fetal/uterine monitors before defibrillation
The gravid uterus compresses the aorta/vena cava, reducing CPR effectiveness
Manual LUD relieves compression better than tilting during compressions
Pregnant patients desaturate quickly and can have a difficult airway
ACLS drug doses should be cut in half during pregnancy
If no ROSC by ~4 minutes, prepare for perimortem cesarean (deliver by ~5)
Consider pregnancy causes: hemorrhage, embolism, eclampsia, Mg toxicity, anesthetic

steps.title: Prioritize Hypotheses
steps.sub: Order the immediate priorities at collapse

opts.text: Call for help / activate the code team and get the crash cart + defibrillator
Start high-quality chest compressions
Provide continuous manual left uterine displacement
Manage the airway with 100% oxygen and attach the defibrillator
Notify OB, anesthesia, and neonatal teams; prepare for perimortem cesarean

steps.title: Generate Solutions
steps.sub: Recommended or Avoid in maternal arrest?

opts.text: Continuous manual left uterine displacement during resuscitation
Compressions at 100–120/min, depth ≥2 in, minimal interruptions
Defibrillate at standard energy when indicated (monitors removed)
Give standard ACLS medications at usual doses
Prepare for perimortem cesarean if no ROSC by ~4 minutes
Give IV calcium gluconate if arrest is from magnesium toxicity
Withhold defibrillation because she is pregnant
Halve the ACLS drug doses for pregnancy
Move the patient to the OR before attempting resuscitation/delivery
opts.rat: Resuscitate and deliver at the bedside.

opts.text: Delay compressions to set up a full left-lateral tilt
opts.rat: Use manual LUD; never delay CPR.

steps.title: Take Action
steps.sub: No ROSC at 4 minutes — sequence the next actions
steps.inst: Rank the order of actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: Continue uninterrupted high-quality CPR with manual LUD
Ensure OB performs perimortem cesarean at the bedside (target ~5 min)
Have the neonatal team ready to resuscitate the newborn
Continue ACLS rhythm checks, medications, and defibrillation as indicated
Reassess for ROSC and reversible causes; document the timeline
The team maintained continuous LUD throughout compressions
Compressions continued without delay while the code team assembled
Standard-dose epinephrine and defibrillation were used
Perimortem cesarean was initiated at the bedside by ~5 minutes
The neonatal team was present and ready for the newborn
Compressions were paused to wait for the OB team
The defibrillator energy was reduced because she was pregnant
The patient was moved to the OR before resuscitation
Fetal monitors were left attached during defibrillation
